name: Coast Guard Base Portsmouth
tagline: The Coast Guard's Atlantic Area headquarters in Virginia's Hampton Roads.
_note: Coast Guard Base Portsmouth (Portsmouth, VIRGINIA - Hampton Roads; NOT Portsmouth NH). CONUS full template (BAH MHA VA298 Norfolk/Portsmouth; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). CG uses same BAH tables. Atlantic Area HQ + LANT commands; cutters. Medical: CG clinic + TRICARE network + nearby DoD MTFs (Naval Medical Center Portsmouth). Facts verified from official sources (uscg.mil, TRICARE, CGX, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Virginia is TRICARE East. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 23704 (Portsmouth).

data.Region: The dense Hampton Roads military area
data.Note: This is Portsmouth, Virginia, not Portsmouth, New Hampshire

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Coast Guard Base Portsmouth is the headquarters of the Coast Guard's Atlantic Area, overseeing operations across the eastern half of the country and beyond, and it homeports cutters.

It's in Portsmouth, Virginia, part of the heavily military Hampton Roads region. The area is used to military families, offers lots of neighborhoods and prices, and has an affordable-for-the-coast cost of living.

sections.title: Hampton Roads Living: Lots of Options

data.subtitle: Hampton Roads is one of the biggest military regions in the country, so you have plenty of choices. The main question is which city fits your commute, schools, and budget.
intro: The region is affordable for a coastal metro and very military-friendly. Coast Guard housing is limited, so most families live off base across the cities.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across Portsmouth, Chesapeake, Suffolk, and the wider Hampton Roads area, with the beaches and history of the region nearby.

recommendations.advice: Portsmouth itself keeps the commute short and has the most affordable housing in the area.
Chesapeake and Suffolk are popular with families for newer homes and good schools.
Virginia Beach is a drive east if you want the oceanfront lifestyle.
Some military housing exists; check early, but plan to live off base.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD/CG ID need to check in at the visitor control point.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Coast Guard site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Coast Guard family housing in Hampton Roads is limited, so most families live off base across the region's cities.
Eligibility and availability vary, so start with the Coast Guard housing office for current options and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA VA298 (Norfolk/Portsmouth) — Coast Guard uses the same BAH tables

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Portsmouth, Chesapeake, and Suffolk districts serve military families depending on where you live; Chesapeake is well regarded.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: On-base clinic care plus access to the large Naval Medical Center Portsmouth and the TRICARE network

data.Commissary: Access to Hampton Roads DoD commissaries (Norfolk and others)

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and in military-heavy, affordable Hampton Roads buying is a real option, especially with repeat tours.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your rotation feels uncertain.
You want time to learn the region and pick the right city.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect a longer tour or repeat Hampton Roads assignments.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The affordable-for-the-coast market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a city and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but the large military presence and affordable market make Hampton Roads a common place to buy. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Check housing
steps.text: CG housing is limited; get the office's read on the Hampton Roads cities before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Virginia vehicle registration, and learn the visitor process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (Portsmouth, Chesapeake, or Suffolk) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment; the base clinic plus Naval Medical Center Portsmouth and the network serve families.

data.a: Portsmouth, Virginia, in Hampton Roads, not Portsmouth, New Hampshire. It's the Coast Guard's Atlantic Area headquarters.

data.q: Do Coast Guard families get BAH?
data.a: Yes, the same BAH tables as the rest of the military. It's MHA VA298 (Norfolk/Portsmouth).
Mostly off base across Hampton Roads. Portsmouth is closest and most affordable; Chesapeake and Suffolk are popular for schools; Virginia Beach for the oceanfront.
Yes, for a coastal metro, and it's very military-friendly, so many families buy.
